step_by_step_execution.description: loc is computed as inner_product of g_truth and output_strides, producing a negative value

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: loc is negative and output_elements is positive, so negative < positive is true. The check passes, failing to catch the out-of-bounds access.

step_by_step_execution.description: output_t(loc) is written with a negative index, causing out-of-bounds write before the buffer

out_state.value: write to memory before the output buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly describes the CVE-2022-29208 vulnerability: the missing lower bound check (0 <= loc) on the inner_product result allows negative indices to pass validation and cause out-of-bounds writes. The fix adds '0 <= loc &&' to all five check sites.

actual_issue_summary: In tf.raw_ops.EditDistance, the computed index 'loc' (from std::inner_product of group indices and output strides) was only checked against the upper bound (loc < output_elements) but not the lower bound (0 <= loc). Negative sparse tensor indices could produce a negative loc, bypassing the check and causing out-of-bounds writes before the output buffer. The fix adds '0 <= loc' checks at all five code sites.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions all TFLite operations that use quantization can be made to use unitialized values. [For example](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/depthwise_conv.cc#L198-L200). The issue stems from the fact that `quantization.params` is only valid if `quantization.type` is different that `kTfLiteNoQuantization`. However, these checks are missing in large parts of the code. We have patched the issue in GitHub commits 537bc7c723439b9194a358f64d871dd326c18887, 4a91f2069f7145aab6ba2d8cfe41be8a110c18a5 and 8933b8a21280696ab119b63263babdb54c298538. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

undesired_operation.description: When params->rank is negative (e.g., -1), num_units becomes negative (num_filters / rank). This negative value is then used as a tensor dimension in output_size_array->data[1] = num_units, which is passed to context->ResizeTensor. A negative dimension can cause memory corruption or unexpected allocation behavior.
undesired_operation.impact: An attacker providing a model with a negative rank parameter can cause a negative tensor dimension to be passed to ResizeTensor, potentially leading to memory corruption or denial of service.

step_by_step_execution.description: rank is read from params->rank

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: rank is -1 which is not 0

step_by_step_execution.description: num_units = num_filters / rank computes a negative value
num_units used as tensor dimension in ResizeTensor

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a hypothetical issue with negative rank values causing negative tensor dimensions. The actual CVE (CVE-2021-37682) is about accessing quantization.params without first checking that quantization.type != kTfLiteNoQuantization, leading to use of uninitialized values. The fix adds TF_LITE_ENSURE checks for quantization.type before accessing .params. The negative rank issue is unrelated to the actual vulnerability.

actual_issue_summary: The vulnerability is that quantization.params is accessed and dereferenced (cast to TfLiteAffineQuantization*) for input, weights_feature, state, weights_time, and output tensors without first checking that quantization.type != kTfLiteNoQuantization. If quantization.type is kTfLiteNoQuantization, the params field is uninitialized/invalid, leading to use of uninitialized data and potential null pointer dereference. The fix adds TF_LITE_ENSURE checks that each tensor's quantization.type != kTfLiteNoQuantization before accessing quantization.params.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in the C language. Versions 2.12 and prior contain a denial-of-service vulnerability that affects PJSIP users that consume PJSIP's XML parsing in their apps. Users are advised to update. There are no known workarounds.

undesired_operation.description: The attribute parsing while-loop dereferences *scanner->curptr without checking for EOF. If the XML input is truncated (e.g., '<tag ') and the scanner functions consume all input, the loop condition only terminates on '>' or '/', but pj_scan_get_until_chr does not advance the scanner when the current character is already a delimiter (space), causing an infinite loop with continuous pool allocations.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service — attacker sends truncated XML like '<tag ' causing the attribute parsing loop to not advance the scanner and loop infinitely, allocating attr objects each iteration until pool memory is exhausted or the process hangs

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE (CVE-2022-24763) is an infinite loop in the CDATA content scanning section. When parsing CDATA, if the scanner encounters a ']' that is not followed by ']>', the while loop calls pj_scan_get_until_ch(scanner, ']', &dummy) without first advancing past the current ']', causing the scanner to never make progress. The fix adds pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE) before that call. Finding 0 describes a different hypothetical infinite loop in the attribute parsing section, which is not what the CVE or the fix addresses.

actual_issue_summary: Infinite loop in CDATA content parsing: when scanning CDATA sections, if a ']' character is found but is not part of the ']]>' closing sequence, the while loop re-scans for ']' without advancing past the current ']', causing the scanner to loop forever. The fix adds pj_scan_advance_n(scanner, 1, PJ_FALSE) to skip past the non-terminating ']'.

cve_desc: Nginx NJS v0.7.3 was discovered to contain a stack overflow in the function njs_default_module_loader at /src/njs/src/njs_module.c. NOTE: multiple third parties dispute this report, e.g., the behavior is only found in unreleased development code that was not part of the 0.7.2, 0.7.3, or 0.7.4 release

undesired_operation.description: When dir is non-NULL, length is set to dir->length (overwriting info->name.length) instead of being incremented by dir->length. The bounds check against NJS_MAX_PATH therefore only considers dir->length, not the total of dir->length + info->name.length. The subsequent njs_cpymem copies info->name into the stack buffer past the space validated by the check, causing a stack buffer overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow allowing potential arbitrary code execution by an attacker who controls the module name length.

state.value: dir->length (+ optional 1 for trail), missing info->name.length component
large enough that dir->length + info->name.length > NJS_MAX_PATH

initial_state.value: uninitialized stack buffer of NJS_MAX_PATH + 1 bytes

step_by_step_execution.description: length is initialized to info->name.length

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: dir is provided as non-NULL by the caller

step_by_step_execution.description: length is assigned (=) dir->length, discarding the previous info->name.length value

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: dir->length alone can be small (e.g., 10), so the check passes even though the combined dir->length + info->name.length exceeds NJS_MAX_PATH

step_by_step_execution.description: dir contents and trailing slash are copied into src
info->name is copied into src starting at offset dir->length+trail, writing dir->length+trail+info->name.length bytes total into a buffer of NJS_MAX_PATH+1 bytes — overflow occurs

out_state.value: overflowed past NJS_MAX_PATH+1 bytes on the stack

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the exact bug fixed by the commit: using assignment (=) instead of addition (+=) for length when dir is non-NULL causes the bounds check to miss info->name.length, leading to a stack buffer overflow via njs_cpymem. This matches CVE-2022-29379 (stack overflow in njs_module.c).

actual_issue_summary: In njs_module_path(), when dir is non-NULL, `length = dir->length` overwrites the previously stored info->name.length instead of adding to it (`length += dir->length`). This means the NJS_MAX_PATH bounds check only validates dir->length, not the total path length. A sufficiently long module name can then overflow the stack buffer `src[NJS_MAX_PATH + 1]` during the njs_cpymem call.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an open source platform for machine learning. Prior to versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4, the implementation of `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeAndDequantizeV4Grad` does not fully validate the input arguments. This results in a `CHECK`-failure which can be used to trigger a denial of service attack. Versions 2.9.0, 2.8.1, 2.7.2, and 2.6.4 contain a patch for this issue.

undesired_operation.description: When axis_ == -1, input_min_tensor.scalar<T>() and input_max_tensor.scalar<T>() are called without verifying that the tensors contain exactly 1 element. An attacker can supply a 1-D min/max tensor with dim_size(0) != 1 (e.g., 0 or >1 elements), bypassing the dims check (which allows dims==1) and the dim_size check (which is gated on axis_ != -1). The scalar<T>() accessor triggers a CHECK failure, crashing the process.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via CHECK-failure crash. An attacker providing crafted inputs to the TensorFlow op can crash the serving process.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: axis_ == -1 satisfies >= -1
axis_ == -1 so the first disjunct is true
Attacker provides matching gradient and input shapes
input_min_tensor is 1-D, so dims()==1 passes the check
input_max_tensor is 1-D, so dims()==1 passes the check
axis_ == -1, so the dim_size checks are skipped entirely
axis_ == -1, entering the scalar code path

step_by_step_execution.description: input_min_tensor.scalar<T>() is called on a non-scalar tensor, triggering CHECK failure

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches CVE-2022-29192. The fix adds OP_REQUIRES checks that input_min and input_max are scalars when axis==-1, before calling .scalar<T>(). The finding correctly identifies that a 1-D tensor with size != 1 bypasses existing validation and triggers the CHECK failure.

actual_issue_summary: When axis_ == -1, the code calls input_min_tensor.scalar<T>() and input_max_tensor.scalar<T>() without first verifying these tensors are actually scalars. A 1-D tensor with dims==1 but dim_size(0) != 1 passes the existing validation (which only checks dims, not num_elements) and the dim_size check is skipped when axis_==-1. This causes a CHECK failure crash (denial of service). The fix adds IsScalar checks before the scalar<T>() calls.

cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.6.0.

undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via crash (NULL pointer dereference). An attacker providing a malformed Mach-O file where get_prelink_info_range_from_mach0 returns NULL triggers this.

get_prelink_info_range_from_mach0 returns NULL for prelink_range

actual_issue_summary: In the beach error handler, obj->cache_buf = NULL dereferences obj without checking if it's NULL. When get_prelink_info_range_from_mach0 returns NULL, obj is still NULL and the goto beach causes a NULL pointer dereference. The fix adds an if (obj) check before the dereference.

cve_desc: OwnTone (aka owntone-server) through 28.1 has a use-after-free in net_bind() in misc.c.

undesired_operation.description: After freeaddrinfo(servinfo) frees the entire addrinfo linked list, the code dereferences ptr (which pointed into that freed list) via getsockname(fd, ptr->ai_addr, &ptr->ai_addrlen). This is a use-after-free: ptr->ai_addr and ptr->ai_addrlen access freed heap memory.
undesired_operation.impact: Use-after-free can lead to crash (denial of service), or potentially arbitrary code execution if the freed memory is reallocated and attacker-controlled data is placed there before the dereference. The getsockname call writes into freed memory (ptr->ai_addr), and subsequent reads from ptr->ai_addr could return corrupted data.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-38383: use-after-free in net_bind() where ptr is used after freeaddrinfo() has freed the memory it points to.

actual_issue_summary: After freeaddrinfo(servinfo) frees the addrinfo linked list, ptr (which points into that list) is still used to call getsockname() and access ai_addr/ai_addrlen. The fix introduces a local union net_sockaddr variable (naddr) to pass to getsockname instead of using the freed ptr.

cve_desc: Use After Free in GitHub repository gpac/gpac prior to 2.1-DEV.

undesired_operation.description: When is_mem_com is true and a QuantizationParameter node is decoded with !codec->force_keep_qp, gf_node_unregister is called with 'node' as the parent, but the new_node was originally registered with NULL as the parent (because is_mem_com is true). This parent mismatch in unregister can corrupt node's child list or decrement its reference count incorrectly, potentially leading to use-after-free.
undesired_operation.impact: Memory corruption or use-after-free — incorrect parent reference counting could free node prematurely or corrupt its child list

step_by_step_execution.description: new_node registered with NULL parent because is_mem_com is true
gf_node_unregister called with node as parent, mismatching the NULL registration

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE is about early returns in the MFNODE loop skipping QP cleanup (gf_bifs_dec_qp_remove), leading to use-after-free of QP nodes. The fix changes 'return e' to 'goto exit' so cleanup always runs. The finding describes a different issue (parent mismatch in register/unregister) that is unrelated to the actual fix and CVE.

actual_issue_summary: In the MFNODE processing branch, early 'return e' on error skipped the QP cleanup code (gf_bifs_dec_qp_remove calls) at the end of the function, leaving stale QP references in the codec that could be used after the referenced nodes were freed. The fix replaces early returns with 'goto exit' to ensure QP cleanup always executes.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C language implementing standard based protocols such as SIP, SDP, RTP, STUN, TURN, and ICE. In versions prior to and including 2.12.1 a stack buffer overflow vulnerability affects PJSIP users that use STUN in their applications, either by: setting a STUN server in their account/media config in PJSUA/PJSUA2 level, or directly using `pjlib-util/stun_simple` API. A patch is available in commit 450baca which should be included in the next release. There are no known workarounds for this issue.

undesired_operation.description: The while loop writes to msg->attr[msg->attr_count] and increments msg->attr_count without any bounds check against the fixed size of the msg->attr array. An attacker can craft a STUN message with many small attributes to overflow the array.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds write past the msg->attr array, potentially overwriting adjacent fields in the pjstun_msg struct or beyond, leading to code execution or crash.

initial_state.value: attacker-controlled, read from STUN header, validated to equal buf_len - sizeof(pjstun_msg_hdr)

step_by_step_execution.description: msg->attr_count is initialized to 0 and p_attr points past the STUN header

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker crafts a STUN message with many small (e.g., 4-byte) attributes so msg_len is large enough for many iterations

step_by_step_execution.description: Each iteration writes to msg->attr[msg->attr_count], advances p_attr, decrements msg_len, and increments attr_count. After PJ_ARRAY_SIZE(msg->attr) iterations, attr_count equals the array size.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: There is no check that msg->attr_count < PJ_ARRAY_SIZE(msg->attr), so the loop continues as long as msg_len > 0. With enough small attributes, msg_len is still positive.

step_by_step_execution.description: The write *attr = (pjstun_attr_hdr*)p_attr writes past the end of the msg->attr array, causing a stack buffer overflow.

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches CVE-2022-31031: unbounded writes to msg->attr[] in the STUN parsing loop due to missing bounds check on attr_count, leading to stack buffer overflow. The fix adds the missing `msg->attr_count < attr_max_cnt` check.

actual_issue_summary: In pjstun_parse_msg(), the while loop parsing STUN attributes increments msg->attr_count and writes to msg->attr[msg->attr_count] without checking that attr_count stays within the fixed-size msg->attr array bounds, allowing a stack buffer overflow via a crafted STUN message with many attributes. The fix adds a bounds check `msg->attr_count < attr_max_cnt` to the loop condition.

cve_desc: An unauthenticated stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in common.c's handle_PORT in uftpd FTP server versions 2.10 and earlier can be abused to cause a crash and could potentially lead to remote code execution.

undesired_operation.description: sprintf writes a formatted IP address string into a 16-byte stack buffer 'addr' (INET_ADDRSTRLEN=16). Because sscanf parses %d with no range restriction, attacker-controlled integers a,b,c,d can be large values (e.g. 2147483647), causing sprintf to write up to ~44 bytes into the 16-byte buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution or crash. Attacker controls the FTP PORT command argument.

state.value: attacker-controlled integers with no range restriction, can be up to 2147483647

initial_state.value: attacker-controlled string from FTP PORT command

step_by_step_execution.description: sscanf parses str with format "%d,%d,%d,%d,%d,%d", reading attacker-controlled integers into a,b,c,d,e,f with no range validation
sprintf formats "%d.%d.%d.%d" into addr, producing "2147483647.2147483647.2147483647.2147483647" (~43 chars + null) into a 16-byte buffer

out_state.value: overflowed: ~44 bytes written into 16-byte buffer, corrupting adjacent stack memory

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly describes the CVE-2020-20276 vulnerability: the use of sprintf without bounds checking allows a stack buffer overflow in the PORT command handler, which was fixed by replacing sprintf with snprintf.

actual_issue_summary: In handle_PORT(), sprintf is used to format an IP address string into a fixed 16-byte stack buffer 'addr'. Since sscanf reads integers without range validation from attacker-controlled input, large integer values can cause sprintf to write beyond the buffer boundary. The fix replaces sprintf with snprintf(addr, sizeof(addr), ...) to prevent the overflow.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. PJSIP versions 2.12 and prior do not parse incoming RTCP feedback RPSI (Reference Picture Selection Indication) packet, but any app that directly uses pjmedia_rtcp_fb_parse_rpsi() will be affected. A patch is available in the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. There are currently no known workarounds.

undesired_operation.description: When padlen (attacker-controlled byte from packet) is larger than rpsi_len*8 - 16, the subtraction `rpsi_len*8 - 16 - padlen` causes an unsigned integer underflow in rpsi->rpsi_bit_len, resulting in a very large value. This causes pj_strset to set an enormous string length, leading to out-of-bounds reads when the string is later accessed.
undesired_operation.impact: Information disclosure via out-of-bounds read or denial of service via crash when the rpsi string (with a huge length) is subsequently accessed by the caller.

state.value: underflowed to a very large value

step_by_step_execution.description: Header is cast and pt/count fields are checked to match RTCP_PSFB/3

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker crafts packet with correct pt and count fields

step_by_step_execution.description: rpsi_len is computed from hdr->length. For example, hdr->length=3 (in network byte order) gives rpsi_len=(3-2)*4=4

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker provides a buffer of sufficient length

step_by_step_execution.description: p is set to point past the header, padlen is read from the first byte. Attacker sets padlen to e.g. 255.
rpsi_bit_len is computed as rpsi_len*8 - 16 - padlen = 32 - 16 - 255. Since rpsi_bit_len is pj_size_t (unsigned), this underflows to a very large value.
pj_strset sets rpsi->rpsi to point at p with length (rpsi->rpsi_bit_len + 7)/8, which is enormous due to underflow

out_state.value: very large value, enabling OOB read

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core vulnerability: lack of validation on padlen allows an integer underflow in rpsi_bit_len, leading to OOB read/write. The fix adds exactly the checks (padlen >= 32 and rpsi_len*8 < 16+padlen) that prevent this. This matches CVE-2022-24786.

actual_issue_summary: Missing validation of header length and padding length fields in pjmedia_rtcp_fb_parse_rpsi() allows crafted RTCP packets to cause integer underflow in rpsi_bit_len calculation (rpsi_len*8 - 16 - padlen), leading to out-of-bounds read/write. The fix adds checks that hdr->length >= 3, padlen < 32, and rpsi_len*8 >= 16 + padlen.

cve_desc: drivers/net/wireless/rsi/rsi_91x_usb.c in the Linux kernel through 5.2.9 has a Double Free via crafted USB device traffic (which may be remote via usbip or usbredir).

undesired_operation.description: After kfree(rsi_dev) in the fail_eps error path, adapter->rsi_dev still points to the freed memory. Any subsequent access to adapter->rsi_dev by the caller or other kernel code constitutes a use-after-free.
undesired_operation.impact: Use-after-free via dangling pointer in adapter->rsi_dev. If the caller or other kernel subsystem accesses adapter->rsi_dev after this function returns an error, it dereferences freed heap memory, potentially leading to code execution or privilege escalation.

step_by_step_execution.description: rsi_dev is allocated via kzalloc
adapter->rsi_dev is set to rsi_dev

out_state.value: same as rsi_dev, valid heap pointer

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Crafted USB device traffic causes endpoint discovery to fail, triggering goto fail_eps

step_by_step_execution.description: kfree(rsi_dev) is called in fail_eps, freeing the memory but adapter->rsi_dev still points to it

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause of CVE-2019-15504: kfree(rsi_dev) in the fail_eps error path leaves adapter->rsi_dev as a dangling pointer. The CVE describes a double free because the caller (rsi_probe) subsequently calls rsi_91x_deinit() which frees adapter->rsi_dev again. The finding identifies the same problematic kfree and the same dangling pointer that enables the double free. While it labels it CWE-416 (use-after-free) instead of CWE-415 (double free), the double free is a specific consequence of the dangling pointer problem correctly identified here. The fix is the same: removing kfree(rsi_dev) from the error path.

actual_issue_summary: In rsi_init_usb_interface, the fail_eps error path calls kfree(rsi_dev) while adapter->rsi_dev still points to it. The caller rsi_probe then calls rsi_91x_deinit() on error, which frees adapter->rsi_dev again, resulting in a double free (CWE-415). The fix removes kfree(rsi_dev) from the error path in rsi_init_usb_interface, letting rsi_91x_deinit handle the cleanup.

cve_desc: scheme/webauthn.c in Glewlwyd SSO server through 2.5.3 has a buffer overflow during FIDO2 signature validation in webauthn registration.

undesired_operation.description: Multiple memcpy calls copy attacker-controlled data into the fixed 200-byte stack buffer 'data_signed' without any bounds checking. The total bytes written are 1 + rpid_hash_len + client_data_hash_len(32) + credential_id_len + 1 + cert_x_len + cert_y_len. If the sum exceeds 200 (e.g., credential_id_len=150, rpid_hash_len=32, cert_x_len=32, cert_y_len=32 yields 280), a stack buffer overflow occurs.
code_snippets: unsigned char data_signed[200], client_data_hash[32], cert_export[32], cert_export_b64[64];
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution. An attacker can supply a crafted authenticator response with a large credential_id to overflow the buffer and overwrite the return address or other stack variables.

step_by_step_execution.description: data_signed[0] is set to 0x0, offset becomes 1
rpid_hash is copied into data_signed at offset 1
client_data_hash (32 bytes) is copied into data_signed
credential_id is copied into data_signed; credential_id_len is attacker-controlled and can be large

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: With credential_id_len=150, offset becomes 215 which exceeds the 200-byte buffer, but no bounds check exists so execution continues and memcpy writes past the buffer boundary

step_by_step_execution.description: Additional copies of cert_x and cert_y further overflow the buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-40818: a buffer overflow in the fixed-size 200-byte stack buffer 'data_signed' during FIDO2 signature validation. The fix replaces the stack buffer with a dynamically allocated buffer sized to fit all components. CWE-121/CWE-120 (stack buffer overflow) is consistent with the NVD-assigned CWE-787 (out-of-bounds write).

actual_issue_summary: The fixed-size 200-byte stack buffer 'data_signed' could be overflowed when building the signature verification bytestring, because the combined length of rpid_hash, client_data_hash, credential_id, cert_x, and cert_y (plus 2 extra bytes) could exceed 200. The fix replaces the stack buffer with a dynamically allocated buffer sized to the actual total length needed.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the drill format T-code tool number functionality of Gerbv 2.7.0, dev (commit b5f1eacd), and the forked version of Gerbv (commit 71493260). A specially-crafted drill file can lead to code execution. An attacker can provide a malicious file to trigger this vulnerability.

undesired_operation.description: When tool_num is outside the valid range (< TOOL_MIN or >= TOOL_MAX), the code logs an error but does NOT return. Execution continues to access image->aperture[tool_num], causing an out-of-bounds read and potentially an out-of-bounds write when assigning a new aperture.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read/write on the image->aperture array. An attacker providing a crafted drill file with an out-of-range tool number can corrupt memory, potentially achieving arbitrary code execution or causing a crash.

step_by_step_execution.description: tool_num is read from the drill file via gerb_fgetint(fd, NULL)

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The attacker provides a non-zero tool number
The attacker-controlled tool_num exceeds TOOL_MAX

step_by_step_execution.description: Error is logged but execution continues (missing return -1)
image->aperture[tool_num] is accessed out of bounds

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches CVE-2021-40391: the missing 'return -1' after the out-of-bounds check allows tool_num values outside the valid range to be used as an array index into image->aperture[], causing an out-of-bounds write. The fix adds exactly that 'return -1'.

actual_issue_summary: In drill_parse_T_code(), when tool_num is outside valid bounds (< TOOL_MIN or >= TOOL_MAX), an error message is logged but execution continues, using the out-of-bounds tool_num as an index into image->aperture[]. The fix adds 'return -1' after logging the error to prevent the out-of-bounds array access.

cve_desc: drivers/bluetooth/virtio_bt.c in the Linux kernel before 5.16.3 has a memory leak (socket buffers have memory allocated but not freed).

undesired_operation.description: When pkt_type does not match any of the four known HCI packet types, the switch statement falls through without freeing skb or passing it to any handler, causing a kernel memory leak.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service — an attacker controlling a virtio-bluetooth device can repeatedly send packets with invalid pkt_type values, leaking sk_buff memory in the kernel until the system is out of memory.

state.value: any value not in {HCI_EVENT_PKT, HCI_ACLDATA_PKT, HCI_SCODATA_PKT, HCI_ISODATA_PKT}

step_by_step_execution.description: pkt_type is read from the first byte of skb->data
skb_pull removes the first byte from skb

out_state.value: allocated sk_buff with data pointer advanced by 1

step_by_step_execution.condition: pkt_type matches HCI_EVENT_PKT, HCI_ACLDATA_PKT, HCI_SCODATA_PKT, or HCI_ISODATA_PKT
step_by_step_execution.reasoning: pkt_type is an invalid value (e.g. 0xFF) that doesn't match any case

step_by_step_execution.description: Switch statement exits without executing any case; skb is never freed

out_state.value: allocated sk_buff, leaked — no reference held, never freed

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly identifies the CVE-2022-26878 vulnerability: missing default case in the switch causes sk_buff memory leak for invalid packet types. CWE-401 (missing free) is equivalent to CWE-772 (missing release).

actual_issue_summary: In virtbt_rx_handle(), when a received packet has an invalid pkt_type that doesn't match any known HCI packet type, the switch statement has no default case, so the sk_buff is never freed, causing a memory leak. The fix adds a default case that calls kfree_skb(skb).

cve_desc: A double-free condition exists in contrib/shpsort.c of shapelib 1.5.0 and older releases. This issue may allow an attacker to cause a denial of service or have other unspecified impact via control over malloc.

undesired_operation.description: Double-free of `copy`: After the for loop, `free(copy)` is called. Then, in the second realloc error path, `free(copy)` is called again on the already-freed pointer.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap corruption leading to potential arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker who can trigger realloc failure at the right time can exploit the double-free.

state.value: already freed by the first free(copy) after the for loop

step_by_step_execution.description: The for loop iterates over tokens, populating result array. Assume at least one token is found so i > 0 after loop.

out_state.value: heap-allocated array of i string pointers

step_by_step_execution.description: free(copy) is called after the for loop ends

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: At least one token was found in the for loop

step_by_step_execution.description: realloc(result, sizeof *result * (i + 1)) is called to add space for NULL terminator, and it fails returning NULL

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: realloc returned NULL indicating failure

step_by_step_execution.description: Error cleanup frees result array elements, frees result, then calls free(copy) on already-freed pointer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-0699: the double-free of `copy` in contrib/shpsort.c. The `copy` pointer is freed after the for loop, then freed again in the second realloc error path. The fix removes the second `free(copy)`.

actual_issue_summary: In the `split` function in contrib/shpsort.c, the `copy` variable is freed after the for loop, but is freed again in the error-handling path of the second realloc call, resulting in a double-free. The fix removes the second `free(copy)`.

cve_desc: A flaw was found in cairo's image-compositor.c in all versions prior to 1.17.4. This flaw allows an attacker who can provide a crafted input file to cairo's image-compositor (for example, by convincing a user to open a file in an application using cairo, or if an application uses cairo on untrusted input) to cause a stack buffer overflow -> out-of-bounds WRITE. The highest impact from this vulnerability is to confidentiality, integrity, as well as system availability.

undesired_operation.description: When a span has coverage that is neither 0xff nor 0x00, the code writes 'len' bytes (spans[1].x - spans[0].x) into the buffer r->_buf via memset without any bounds check against the buffer size. If len is large (e.g. len >= run_length but coverage != 0xff), or multiple such spans accumulate, the write overflows r->_buf.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution or denial of service. An attacker who controls the span data (e.g., via a crafted vector graphic) can write arbitrary byte values past the end of r->_buf.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core vulnerability: writing span coverage data into r->_buf (which is a stack buffer, per CVE description) without bounds checking, leading to an out-of-bounds write. The fix replaces r->_buf with the properly-sized mask pixel data buffer obtained via pixman_image_get_data(r->mask). The finding says 'heap' buffer overflow while the CVE says 'stack', but it correctly identifies the same out-of-bounds write vulnerability in image-compositor.c and the exact code location where the overflow occurs (the memset and pointer increment writing into r->_buf). This matches CVE-2020-35492 / CWE-787.

actual_issue_summary: The function _inplace_src_spans incorrectly used r->_buf (a small stack buffer) instead of the mask image's pixel data buffer for writing span coverage values. When spans with partial coverage were processed, coverage bytes were written into r->_buf which could overflow since it's not sized for the full span data. The fix replaces r->_buf with the proper mask data buffer obtained via pixman_image_get_data(r->mask).

cve_desc: Use-After-Free in str_escape in mruby/mruby in GitHub repository mruby/mruby prior to 3.2. Possible arbitrary code execution if being exploited.

undesired_operation.description: In OP_ARGARY, when kd=1 and lv!=0, the bounds check on the environment length is insufficient, allowing out-of-bounds access to stack[m1+r+m2+1].
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from environment stack, potentially leaking sensitive data or causing a crash.

step_by_step_execution.description: OP_ARGARY decodes b to extract m1, r, m2, kd, lv

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: If MRB_ENV_LEN(e) == m1+r+m2+2, the check passes

step_by_step_execution.description: With kd=1, accesses stack[m1+r+m2+1] which is e->stack[m1+r+m2+2], one past the valid length

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential OOB read in OP_ARGARY bounds checking, which is unrelated to CVE-2022-1212. The CVE is about a use-after-free caused by creating a break object after clearing the GC arena in the OP_R_BREAK handler. The fix reorders break_new() to occur before mrb_gc_arena_restore().

undesired_operation.description: In OP_BLKPUSH, when kd=1 and lv!=0, the bounds check is insufficient allowing out-of-bounds access.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from environment stack, potentially leaking sensitive data or causing a crash.

step_by_step_execution.description: OP_BLKPUSH decodes b to extract m1, r, m2, kd, lv

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: If MRB_ENV_LEN(e) == m1+r+m2+2, the check passes

step_by_step_execution.description: With kd=1, accesses stack[m1+r+m2+1] which is e->stack[m1+r+m2+2], one past the valid length

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential OOB read in OP_BLKPUSH, unrelated to CVE-2022-1212. The CVE is a use-after-free where break_new() is called after mrb_gc_arena_restore(), causing the newly created break object to potentially be garbage collected.

actual_issue_summary: In the OP_R_BREAK handler, when ci->cci > CINFO_NONE, the code called mrb_gc_arena_restore() before break_new(). This meant the break object created by break_new() was not protected by the GC arena and could be freed prematurely, causing a use-after-free. The fix reorders the operations so break_new() is called before mrb_gc_arena_restore(), ensuring the break object is in the arena and protected from GC.

cve_desc: The ip6gre_err function in net/ipv6/ip6_gre.c in the Linux kernel allows remote attackers to have unspecified impact via vectors involving GRE flags in an IPv6 packet, which trigger an out-of-bounds access.

undesired_operation.description: Out-of-bounds read when looking up the GRE key. The expression `((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1` uses grehlen which includes the offset from the start of skb->data, but p already points to skb->data + offset. This double-counts offset, causing a read beyond guaranteed skb linear data.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from skb data. An attacker sending a crafted ICMPv6 error message for a GRE-in-IPv6 tunnel can read kernel memory beyond the guaranteed skb linear data, potentially causing information disclosure or a kernel crash.

state.value: offset + 4 (or more), includes IPv6 header offset (~40 bytes extra)
skb->data + offset (already past the IPv6 headers)

initial_state.value: offset into skb->data where GRE header starts (includes IPv6 header size, ~40 bytes)

step_by_step_execution.description: p is set to point at the GRE header within skb->data
grehlen is initialized to offset + 4, including the IPv6 header offset
flags is read from p[0] (GRE flags field)

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: GRE_KEY flag is set
Neither GRE_VERSION nor GRE_ROUTING is set
GRE_KEY flag is set

step_by_step_execution.description: grehlen is incremented by 4 for GRE_KEY; GRE_CSUM not set so no further increment
pskb_may_pull ensures grehlen bytes are available, then p is recalculated to skb->data + offset

GRE key lookup: expression ((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1 computes p + (offset+8)/4 - 1. Since p = skb->data + offset, this reads at skb->data + offset + 4*(grehlen/4 - 1) = skb->data + offset + 4*((offset+8)/4 - 1). The offset (~40) is double-counted, reading ~40 bytes past the actual GRE key location.

out_state.value: ~40 bytes past the intended GRE key field, out-of-bounds

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core issue of CVE-2017-5897: grehlen includes the offset (IPv6 header size), but p already points past that offset. The expression `((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1` therefore double-counts the offset, causing an out-of-bounds read ~40 bytes past the intended location. This matches the CVE description, the commit message, and the CWE-125 classification.

actual_issue_summary: In ip6gre_err(), grehlen was initialized to `offset + 4` (including IPv6 header offset), but was used in the key-extraction expression `((__be32 *)p) + (grehlen / 4) - 1` where p already pointed to `skb->data + offset`. This double-counted the offset, causing an out-of-bounds read ~40 bytes past the actual GRE key location. The fix changes grehlen to track only the GRE header length and uses a separate key_off variable to correctly locate the key.

cve_desc: An out-of-bounds read in the BGP daemon of FRRouting FRR before 8.4 may lead to a segmentation fault and denial of service. This occurs in bgp_capability_msg_parse in bgpd/bgp_packet.c.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy copies sizeof(struct capability) bytes from pnt+1 into a stack variable before validating that enough data remains in the buffer. The prior check only ensures 3 bytes are available, but sizeof(struct capability) is larger.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from attacker-controlled network packet data, leading to crash/denial of service or information disclosure.

state.value: could be as small as 2 bytes
larger than 2 (includes mpc sub-struct with afi/safi fields)

initial_state.value: pointer to start of attacker-controlled capability message data
uninitialized stack variable of type struct capability

step_by_step_execution.description: Enter while loop, pnt < end holds

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: With length=3, pnt+3 == end, so the check passes (not >). We have exactly 3 bytes.

step_by_step_execution.description: action is read from *pnt, then memcpy(&cap, pnt+1, sizeof(struct capability)) copies sizeof(struct capability) bytes starting at pnt+1. Only 2 bytes remain (end - pnt - 1 = 2), but sizeof(struct capability) is larger.

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly describes the CVE-2022-37032 vulnerability: the memcpy of sizeof(struct capability) bytes occurs before the capability length validation, causing an out-of-bounds read. The fix moves the memcpy after the length check and only copies the smaller capability_mp_data struct.

actual_issue_summary: In bgp_capability_msg_parse, memcpy(&cap, pnt+1, sizeof(struct capability)) is performed before validating cap.length, reading potentially past the end of the input buffer. The fix restructures to use a pointer cast for the header and only memcpy the smaller capability_mp_data after length validation. Additionally, afi/safi validation via bgp_afi_safi_valid_indices was added to prevent out-of-bounds array access.

cve_desc: Rizin is a UNIX-like reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset. In versions up to and including 0.3.1 there is a heap-based out of bounds write in parse_die() when reversing an AMD64 ELF binary with DWARF debug info. When a malicious AMD64 ELF binary is opened by a victim user, Rizin may crash or execute unintended actions. No workaround are known and users are advised to upgrade.

undesired_operation.description: When abbrev->count is 0, the expression 'abbrev->count - 1' underflows because count is size_t (unsigned). This results in a loop bound of SIZE_MAX, causing massive out-of-bounds reads and writes on die->attr_values[i] and abbrev->defs[i].
code_snippets: buf = parse_attr_value(buf, buf_end - buf, &abbrev->defs[i], &die->attr_values[i], hdr, debug_str, debug_str_len, big_endian);
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds heap read/write leading to crash or arbitrary code execution when processing a malformed DWARF binary with an abbreviation declaration that has count == 0.

step_by_step_execution.description: Loop condition is evaluated: i < abbrev->count - 1, where abbrev->count is 0 (size_t). The subtraction 0 - 1 underflows to SIZE_MAX.

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: 0 < SIZE_MAX is true, so the loop body executes

step_by_step_execution.description: memset writes to die->attr_values[0], which may be within bounds initially, but subsequent iterations access die->attr_values[1], [2], etc. far beyond allocated size, causing heap-based out-of-bounds write.

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies the CVE-2021-43814 vulnerability: when abbrev->count is 0, the unsigned subtraction underflows causing a massive loop that writes out of bounds on the heap. The fix adds an `if (abbrev->count)` guard, exactly matching this finding.

actual_issue_summary: When abbrev->count is 0, the loop condition `i < abbrev->count - 1` causes an unsigned integer underflow (0 - 1 = SIZE_MAX for size_t), leading to a heap-based out-of-bounds write via memset and parse_attr_value on die->attr_values[i]. The fix wraps the loop in an `if (abbrev->count)` check.

cve_desc: Integer signedness error in the dynamicGetbuf function in gd_io_dp.c in the GD Graphics Library (aka libgd) through 2.2.3, as used in PHP before 5.6.28 and 7.x before 7.0.13, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (stack-based buffer overflow) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted imagecreatefromstring call.

undesired_operation.description: When dp->pos > dp->logicalSize, 'remain' becomes negative. The negative remain bypasses the 'remain == 0' check, so rlen is set to the negative remain value. When passed to memcpy, the negative int rlen is implicitly cast to size_t (a very large unsigned value), causing a massive heap buffer over-read.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer over-read leading to information disclosure or crash (denial of service). An attacker could read sensitive heap data beyond the dp->data buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: remain is computed as dp->logicalSize - dp->pos

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: remain is negative, so it is less than len (a positive requested length)
remain is -50, not 0, so the early return is bypassed

step_by_step_execution.description: rlen is assigned the negative remain value
memcpy is called with rlen=-50, which is cast to size_t (a very large value), causing buffer over-read/overflow

out_state.value: -50 (interpreted as massive unsigned size by memcpy)

judgments.reasoning: This finding correctly identifies the CVE-2016-8670 vulnerability: a negative 'remain' value bypasses the 'remain == 0' check, causing rlen to be negative, which when cast to size_t in memcpy causes a stack/heap buffer overflow. The fix changes 'remain == 0' to 'remain <= 0' to catch negative values. The CWE differs slightly (CWE-122/126 vs CWE-119) but the underlying issue is the same integer signedness error leading to buffer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: Integer signedness error in dynamicGetbuf: when dp->pos > dp->logicalSize, 'remain' becomes negative. The check 'remain == 0' fails to catch negative values, so a negative rlen is passed to memcpy, where it's implicitly converted to a very large size_t, causing a stack-based buffer overflow. The fix changes 'remain == 0' to 'remain <= 0'.

cve_desc: In Singular before 4.3.1, a predictable /tmp pathname is used (e.g., by sdb.cc), which allows local users to gain the privileges of other users via a procedure in a file under /tmp. NOTE: this CVE Record is about sdb.cc and similar files in the Singular interface that have predictable /tmp pathnames; this CVE Record is not about the lack of a safe temporary-file creation capability in the Singular language.

undesired_operation.description: sprintf writes a PID string into filename buffer starting at offset 7, but the buffer was allocated to hold only 14 bytes (strlen("/tmp/sd000000") + 1). On systems where PIDs can be 7 digits (e.g., up to 4194304 on 64-bit Linux), this writes 8 bytes (7 digits + null) at offset 7, requiring 15 bytes total, overflowing the 14-byte buffer by 1 byte.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow by 1 byte, potentially corrupting heap metadata, leading to crash or arbitrary code execution.

step_by_step_execution.description: filename is allocated as a duplicate of "/tmp/sd000000" (14 bytes including null terminator)
sprintf writes PID as decimal string starting at filename+7

out_state.value: 15 bytes needed (offset 7 + 7 digits + null), overflows 14-byte buffer by 1

judgments.reasoning: The CVE is about predictable /tmp pathnames enabling local privilege escalation via symlink attacks (insecure temporary file creation). The finding describes a potential heap buffer overflow from large PIDs, which is a completely different class of vulnerability. The fix replaced the predictable PID-based filename with mkstemp to create unpredictable temporary files securely, not to fix a buffer overflow.

actual_issue_summary: The function used a predictable temporary file path based on PID (/tmp/sd<PID>) which allows local attackers to perform symlink attacks and gain privileges of other users. The fix replaces the predictable path with mkstemp() which creates a unique, unpredictable temporary file securely.

cve_desc: A memory leak in the cx23888_ir_probe() function in drivers/media/pci/cx23885/cx23888-ir.c in the Linux kernel through 5.3.11 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by triggering kfifo_alloc() failures, aka CID-a7b2df76b42b.

undesired_operation.description: When kfifo_alloc fails, the function returns -ENOMEM without freeing the previously allocated 'state', causing a memory leak.
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service through kernel memory exhaustion by repeatedly triggering the allocation failure path.

state.value: valid pointer to allocated cx23888_ir_state struct

step_by_step_execution.description: state is allocated via kzalloc

out_state.value: valid pointer to allocated cx23888_ir_state struct

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: kzalloc succeeded, state is non-NULL

step_by_step_execution.description: spin_lock_init is called on state->rx_kfifo_lock

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: kfifo_alloc fails (e.g., due to memory pressure), returning non-zero

step_by_step_execution.description: Function returns -ENOMEM without calling kfree(state), leaking the allocated memory

out_state.value: leaked - no reference retained, never freed

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2019-19054: a memory leak of 'state' when kfifo_alloc fails in cx23888_ir_probe. The fix adds kfree(state) before returning -ENOMEM.

actual_issue_summary: In cx23888_ir_probe(), when kfifo_alloc() fails, the function returns -ENOMEM without freeing the previously kzalloc'd 'state' structure, causing a memory leak. The fix adds kfree(state) before the return.

cve_desc: A flaw null pointer dereference in the Linux kernel UDF file system functionality was found in the way user triggers udf_file_write_iter function for the malicious UDF image. A local user could use this flaw to crash the system. Actual from Linux kernel 4.2-rc1 till 5.17-rc2.

undesired_operation.description: In the error recovery path (when filemap_fdatawrite fails), the code copies inode->i_size bytes from page data back into iinfo->i_data + iinfo->i_lenEAttr. The inode->i_size could exceed the remaining space in iinfo->i_data buffer (which is typically limited to the inode block size minus i_lenEAttr), causing a buffer overflow of the inline inode data area.
undesired_operation.impact: Kernel heap buffer overflow when error recovery is triggered, potentially leading to privilege escalation or denial of service.

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2022-0617 is about a null pointer dereference (CWE-476) caused by i_lenAlloc not being restored when inode expansion fails. The fix adds `iinfo->i_lenAlloc = inode->i_size;` in the error path. The mismatch between i_lenAlloc (left as 0) and i_size causes null pointer dereferences later in udf_file_write_iter. The finding incorrectly identifies a buffer overflow in the memcpy during error recovery, which is a different issue from the actual CVE.

actual_issue_summary: When udf_expand_file_adinicb fails during filemap_fdatawrite, the error recovery path restores the inode to inline format but forgets to restore i_lenAlloc (which was set to 0 earlier). This mismatch between i_lenAlloc=0 and actual i_size causes null pointer dereferences later when the inode is used (e.g., in udf_file_write_iter). The fix adds `iinfo->i_lenAlloc = inode->i_size;` in the error path.

cve_desc: A race condition exists in Eternal Terminal prior to version 6.2.0 that allows an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization socket, enabling the attacker to login to other systems as the targeted users. The bug is in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId().

undesired_operation.description: strcpy copies attacker-controlled pipePath into fixed-size local.sun_path (typically 108 bytes on Linux) without bounds checking, causing a stack buffer overflow
undesired_operation.impact: Stack buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker who controls the endpoint name can overwrite the return address and other stack variables.

state.value: attacker-controlled string longer than sizeof(local.sun_path) (~108 bytes)

step_by_step_execution.description: pipePath is set from endpoint.name()

out_state.value: string from endpoint, potentially longer than 108 bytes

step_by_step_execution.description: strcpy copies pipePath into local.sun_path without length check

out_state.value: overflowed beyond 108 bytes into adjacent stack memory

judgments.reasoning: CVE-2022-24950 is specifically about a race condition in UserTerminalRouter::getInfoForId() that allows SSH authorization socket hijacking due to missing mutex locks. The finding describes a strcpy buffer overflow in PipeSocketHandler::listen(), which is a different issue that happened to be fixed in the same commit but is not the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2022-24950 is a race condition in UserTerminalRouter where acceptNewConnection() and getInfoForId() lacked mutex protection (routerMutex), allowing an authenticated attacker to hijack other users' SSH authorization sockets. The fix adds lock_guard<recursive_mutex> to both methods. The strcpy→strncpy change in PipeSocketHandler was a separate hardening fix in the same commit.

cve_desc: In the Linux kernel before 5.3.6, there is a use-after-free bug that can be caused by a malicious USB device in the drivers/net/ieee802154/atusb.c driver, aka CID-7fd25e6fc035.

undesired_operation.description: After ieee802154_free_hw(atusb->hw) frees the hw structure (which includes the atusb private data allocated by ieee802154_alloc_hw), the code accesses atusb->usb_dev in usb_put_dev(), resulting in a use-after-free.
undesired_operation.impact: Use-after-free can lead to kernel crash (denial of service) or potential privilege escalation if an attacker can reclaim the freed memory with controlled content before the dereference.

state.value: points to freed memory (private data within hw, freed by ieee802154_free_hw)

initial_state.value: valid pointer to private data within atusb->hw, obtained via usb_get_intfdata(interface)

step_by_step_execution.description: ieee802154_unregister_hw(atusb->hw) unregisters the hardware device
ieee802154_free_hw(atusb->hw) frees the hw structure, which includes the atusb private data
usb_put_dev(atusb->usb_dev) dereferences the now-freed atusb pointer to access usb_dev

judgments.reasoning: The finding precisely describes the CVE-2019-19525 vulnerability: ieee802154_free_hw frees atusb (the hw private data), and then usb_put_dev(atusb->usb_dev) accesses the freed memory. The fix moves usb_put_dev before ieee802154_free_hw, exactly matching what the commit addresses.

actual_issue_summary: In atusb_disconnect, ieee802154_free_hw(atusb->hw) frees the hw structure containing the atusb private data, but then usb_put_dev(atusb->usb_dev) dereferences the now-freed atusb pointer. The fix reorders the calls so usb_put_dev executes before ieee802154_free_hw.

cve_desc: Heap Buffer Overflow in parseDragons in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.6.8.

undesired_operation.description: memcmp reads up to 12 bytes (b+8 through b+11) from heap buffer 'b' that may be allocated with as few as 1 byte, causing a heap buffer over-read
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer over-read leading to information disclosure or crash (denial of service). An attacker who controls the binary file can craft a file where the remaining data after 'off' is less than 12 bytes.

step_by_step_execution.description: size is assigned from r_buf_size(buf)

out_state.value: total size of buf, e.g. off + 5

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: off is less than size so we proceed

step_by_step_execution.description: size -= off, reducing size to remaining bytes

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: size is 5, not zero

step_by_step_execution.description: b = malloc(size) allocates a 5-byte buffer
memcmp reads 4 bytes at b+8, which is beyond the 5-byte buffer

out_state.value: heap buffer of 5 bytes, accessed out of bounds at offset 8-11

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core issue: when size (remaining bytes) is small (< 12), the memcmp at b+8 reads past the allocated heap buffer. The fix adds a 'size < 32' check to prevent this. While the CVE is classified as CWE-787 (heap buffer overflow/write), the actual fix addresses insufficient size validation, and the finding correctly identifies the heap buffer access beyond bounds. The read at b+8 with a small buffer is a direct consequence of the missing size check that was added in the fix.

actual_issue_summary: The parseDragons function did not check that the remaining buffer size was at least 32 bytes before performing memcmp operations at offsets up to b+12 and r_read_le32 at b+12, leading to heap buffer over-read/overflow when the buffer was smaller than expected. The fix changes the size type to st64 (signed) and adds a 'size < 32' minimum size check.

cve_desc: The php_wddx_pop_element function in ext/wddx/wddx.c in PHP before 5.6.25 and 7.x before 7.0.10 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via an invalid base64 binary value, as demonstrated by a wddx_deserialize call that mishandles a binary element in a wddxPacket XML document.

undesired_operation.description: php_base64_decode can return NULL on failure (e.g., invalid base64 input). The return value is assigned to Z_STRVAL_P(ent1->data) without a NULL check. This NULL string value in the zval will be dereferenced later when the data is used (e.g., in string operations, hash insertions, or when the zval is eventually destroyed/used by consumer code).
undesired_operation.impact: Denial of service via NULL pointer dereference crash. An attacker who can supply crafted WDDX input with an invalid base64-encoded binary element can crash the PHP process.

state.value: NULL (php_base64_decode returned NULL for invalid base64 input)

initial_state.value: non-NULL zval containing string data from binary element

step_by_step_execution.description: php_base64_decode is called with the binary element's string value, which is invalid base64 data

in_state.value: length of the invalid base64 string

step_by_step_execution.description: STR_FREE frees the old string value of ent1->data
NULL new_str is assigned to Z_STRVAL_P(ent1->data) without any NULL check
new_len (0) is assigned to Z_STRLEN_P(ent1->data)

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2016-7130: a NULL pointer dereference in php_wddx_pop_element due to missing NULL check on the return value of php_base64_decode when processing invalid base64 binary values in WDDX deserialization. The CWE, impact, and code location all align with the CVE and the fix.

actual_issue_summary: php_base64_decode() can return NULL when given invalid base64 input. The return value was assigned directly to Z_STRVAL_P(ent1->data) without checking for NULL, leading to a NULL pointer dereference. The fix adds a NULL check and sets an empty string instead when decoding fails.

cve_desc: A buffer overflow vulnerability was found in the Linux kernel Intel’s iSMT SMBus host controller driver in the way it handled the I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_PROC_CALL case (via the ioctl I2C_SMBUS) with malicious input data. This flaw could allow a local user to crash the system.

undesired_operation.description: In the I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_PROC_CALL case, memcpy(&dma_buffer[1], &data->block[1], data->block[0]) is called without any bounds check on data->block[0]. Since data->block[0] can be up to 255 and dma_buffer points into a small fixed-size buffer, this overflows the buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Kernel heap/stack buffer overflow leading to memory corruption, potential privilege escalation or denial of service

state.value: 0-255 (user controlled, can exceed I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_MAX which is 32)

initial_state.value: PTR_ALIGN(&priv->buffer[0], 16), pointing into a fixed-size buffer

step_by_step_execution.description: Function entry, dma_buffer is set to PTR_ALIGN(&priv->buffer[0], 16)

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The user invokes ioctl with I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_PROC_CALL transaction type

step_by_step_execution.description: memcpy(&dma_buffer[1], &data->block[1], data->block[0]) copies data->block[0] bytes into dma_buffer starting at offset 1, with no bounds check on data->block[0]

in_state.value: points to small fixed-size buffer (~48 bytes)

out_state.value: up to 255 bytes written starting at offset 1, overflowing the buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-3077: the missing bounds check on data->block[0] in the I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_PROC_CALL case allows a buffer overflow via memcpy. The fix adds exactly this check (data->block[0] > I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_MAX).

actual_issue_summary: In the I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_PROC_CALL case of ismt_access(), data->block[0] (user-controlled, 0-255) was used as the length for memcpy into dma_buffer without being capped to I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_MAX (32), causing a buffer overflow. The fix adds a check returning -EINVAL if data->block[0] > I2C_SMBUS_BLOCK_MAX.

cve_desc: st21nfca_connectivity_event_received in drivers/nfc/st21nfca/se.c in the Linux kernel through 5.16.12 has EVT_TRANSACTION buffer overflows because of untrusted length parameters.

undesired_operation.description: memcpy copies transaction->params_len bytes (attacker-controlled, up to 255) from skb data into transaction->params. Even when skb->len passes the weak validation, params_len is read from skb->data[aid_len+3] without any bounds check, and the total copied data (aid + params + struct overhead) can exceed the allocation of skb->len - 2 bytes.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to potential arbitrary code execution or kernel crash. An attacker controlling NFC event data can overflow the transaction buffer via the params_len field.

state.value: attacker-controlled, up to 255, read from skb->data[aid_len+3] without bounds check
skb->len - 2, which may be smaller than struct overhead + aid_len + params_len

step_by_step_execution.description: event matches ST21NFCA_EVT_TRANSACTION, entering that case

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Attacker provides skb->len >= NFC_MIN_AID_LENGTH + 2 so the first conjunct is false, making the whole condition false; validation is bypassed

step_by_step_execution.description: transaction is allocated with size skb->len - 2
transaction->aid_len is read from skb->data[1] (attacker-controlled)
memcpy copies aid_len bytes into transaction->aid — also unbounded but separate issue
transaction->params_len is read from skb->data[aid_len+3], attacker-controlled, up to 255

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: No such check exists in vulnerable code; params_len can exceed remaining allocation space

step_by_step_execution.description: memcpy writes params_len bytes into transaction->params, overflowing the heap allocation

in_state.value: up to 255, larger than remaining buffer space

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the params_len buffer overflow in EVT_TRANSACTION, which is one of the two unchecked length parameters fixed by the commit. This matches CVE-2022-26490.

actual_issue_summary: In st21nfca_connectivity_event_received, both transaction->aid_len and transaction->params_len are read directly from untrusted skb->data without bounds validation before being used as memcpy length arguments, causing heap buffer overflows. The fix adds checks that aid_len doesn't exceed sizeof(transaction->aid) and params_len doesn't exceed the remaining allocated buffer size.

cve_desc: Integer Overflow or Wraparound in GitHub repository vim/vim prior to 9.0.

undesired_operation.description: When the mouse_index_found fallback path is taken (idx == tc_len, mouse_index_found >= 0), the variable idx remains set to tc_len (one past the last valid index of the termcodes[] array). This out-of-bounds idx is passed to check_termcode_mouse(), which may access termcodes[idx] out of bounds.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read from the termcodes[] array via idx = tc_len passed to check_termcode_mouse. May lead to crash or information disclosure from heap memory.

step_by_step_execution.description: Loop iterates through all termcodes without finding a non-mouse match

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: All termcodes checked, mouse match found as fallback

step_by_step_execution.description: key_name set from mouse_index_found but idx remains tc_len

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential issue with idx being out of bounds in the mouse fallback path. This is unrelated to CVE-2022-2285, which is about reading past the end of the typeahead buffer due to a missing NUL terminator. The fix adds `tp[len] = NUL;` to prevent functions like getdigits/atoi from reading beyond valid typeahead data.

undesired_operation.description: When the mouse_index_found fallback path is taken, slen contains the wrong value (from the last loop iteration, not the matched 2-byte mouse termcode). This incorrect slen is passed to put_string_in_typebuf(offset, slen, ...), which replaces slen bytes from the type buffer with new_slen bytes, corrupting valid input data.
code_snippets:
if (put_string_in_typebuf(offset, slen, string, new_slen,
						 buf, bufsize, buflen) == FAIL)
undesired_operation.impact: Type buffer corruption: put_string_in_typebuf removes slen bytes from the type buffer and inserts new_slen replacement bytes. With an inflated slen, this overwrites or removes valid subsequent input data, potentially leading to buffer over-read during the memmove or processing of corrupted command sequences.

state.value: wrong value from last loop iteration

step_by_step_execution.description: slen is set to termcodes[idx].len each iteration

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: Fallback path taken, slen not corrected

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a potential issue with incorrect slen in the mouse fallback path. This is unrelated to CVE-2022-2285, which is about the typeahead buffer not being NUL-terminated, causing functions to read past the end of valid data. The actual fix adds `tp[len] = NUL;` to prevent out-of-bounds reads by string processing functions.

actual_issue_summary: The typeahead buffer was not NUL-terminated at position tp[len], causing functions that process terminal codes (like getdigits(), atoi(), and character-scanning loops) to read past the end of valid typeahead data. The fix adds `tp[len] = NUL;` to ensure a NUL terminator is placed after the valid data, preventing out-of-bounds reads that could lead to integer overflow/wraparound or heap buffer over-read.

cve_desc: This affects the package juce-framework/JUCE before 6.1.5. This vulnerability is triggered when a malicious archive is crafted with an entry containing a symbolic link. When extracted, the symbolic link is followed outside of the target dir allowing writing arbitrary files on the target host. In some cases, this can allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code. The vulnerable code is in the ZipFile::uncompressEntry function in juce_ZipFile.cpp and is executed when the archive is extracted upon calling uncompressTo() on a ZipFile object.

undesired_operation.description: getChildFile is called with an attacker-controlled zip entry filename that may contain '../' path traversal sequences, allowing file writes outside the target directory (Zip Slip attack)
undesired_operation.impact: Arbitrary file write outside the intended extraction directory, potentially leading to code execution by overwriting executables, libraries, or configuration files

state.value: contains '../' sequences allowing path traversal outside targetDirectory

initial_state.value: unset, will be derived from zei->entry.filename which is attacker-controlled

step_by_step_execution.description: entryPath is set from the zip entry filename, which is attacker-controlled

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The attacker provides a non-empty filename

step_by_step_execution.description: targetFile is computed by joining targetDirectory with the attacker-controlled entryPath

out_state.value: /safe/extract/dir/../../etc/malicious which resolves to /safe/etc/malicious or similar path outside targetDirectory

step_by_step_execution.description: No check exists to verify targetFile is within targetDirectory — the vulnerable code proceeds to write or create symlink at the traversed path

out_state.value: path outside targetDirectory, file written or symlink created here

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core vulnerability in CVE-2021-23521: the lack of path validation after getChildFile allows zip entries with '../' traversal sequences (or symbolic links) to write files outside the target directory. The fix adds exactly the missing `isAChildOf` check. While the CVE description emphasizes symbolic links, path traversal is the broader issue and CWE-22 is closely related to CWE-59.

actual_issue_summary: The ZipFile::uncompressEntry function lacked validation that the resolved target file path remained within the target extraction directory. A malicious zip archive could contain entries with path traversal sequences (../) or symbolic links that cause files to be written outside the intended directory. The fix adds an isAChildOf check to ensure the target file stays within the target directory, and also adds a check to prevent following symbolic links in parent directory paths.

cve_desc: MariaDB Server v10.6 and below was discovered to contain an use-after-free in the component my_strcasecmp_8bit, which is exploited via specially crafted SQL statements.

undesired_operation.description: When dl->length is smaller than so_ext_len, the expression 'dl->str + dl->length - so_ext_len' computes a pointer before the start of dl->str buffer, causing an out-of-bounds read in my_strcasecmp.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds read leading to crash (denial of service) or information disclosure. An attacker who can control the plugin name (e.g. via INSTALL PLUGIN with a short name) can trigger this.

initial_state.value: attacker-controlled, can be 0 or very small

step_by_step_execution.description: so_ext_len is computed as sizeof(SO_EXT) - 1
The expression dl->str + dl->length - so_ext_len is computed. With dl->length < so_ext_len, this underflows and points before dl->str.
my_strcasecmp reads from the underflowed pointer, accessing memory before dl->str buffer

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause fixed by the commit: missing bounds check on dl->length before computing dl->str + dl->length - so_ext_len. The CVE description mentions use-after-free in my_strcasecmp_8bit (which is the function called by my_strcasecmp), but the actual issue is accessing memory before the buffer due to unsigned integer underflow in pointer arithmetic. The finding's CWE-125 (out-of-bounds read) is arguably more precise than the CVE's CWE-416, but it describes the same underlying vulnerability fixed by adding 'dl->length < so_ext_len' guard.

actual_issue_summary: When dl->length is smaller than so_ext_len, the pointer arithmetic 'dl->str + dl->length - so_ext_len' underflows and produces an out-of-bounds pointer passed to my_strcasecmp_8bit. The fix adds a check 'dl->length < so_ext_len' before the subtraction to prevent this.

cve_desc: A buffer overflow in the GmfOpenMesh() function of libMeshb v7.61 allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) via a crafted MESH file.

undesired_operation.description: fscanf with %s format specifier reads an unbounded string from a file into the fixed-size stack buffer 'str[GmfStrSiz]', causing a stack buffer overflow when a token in the file exceeds GmfStrSiz-1 bytes.
undesired_operation.impact: Stack-based buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. An attacker who controls the input .mesh/.sol file can craft a token longer than GmfStrSiz bytes to overwrite the return address and gain code execution.

state.value: fixed-size buffer of GmfStrSiz bytes on the stack

initial_state.value: uninitialized stack buffer of GmfStrSiz bytes
open file handle to a crafted .mesh file

step_by_step_execution.description: File is opened for reading in ASCII mode

out_state.value: valid FILE* to the crafted .mesh file

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: The file has .mesh extension (not .meshb), so typ is Asc | MshFil, and the Bin bit is not set.

step_by_step_execution.description: fscanf(msh->hdl, "%s", str) is called in a loop to find 'MeshVersionFormatted'. With a crafted file containing a token longer than GmfStrSiz bytes, the unbounded %s writes past the end of str.

in_state.value: contains a token exceeding GmfStrSiz bytes

out_state.value: overflowed beyond GmfStrSiz bytes, corrupting adjacent stack memory

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches the CVE: a buffer overflow in GmfOpenMesh via unbounded fscanf("%s") into a fixed-size buffer. The fix changes "%s" to "%100s" to limit the read, which is precisely what the diff shows.

actual_issue_summary: Two calls to fscanf(msh->hdl, "%s", str) in the ASCII parsing path of GmfOpenMesh() use an unbounded %s format specifier to read into the fixed-size stack buffer str[GmfStrSiz]. A crafted .mesh file with an overly long token causes a stack buffer overflow. The fix limits the read to 100 characters by changing the format to "%100s".

cve_desc: LibVNC before commit 7b1ef0ffc4815cab9a96c7278394152bdc89dc4d contains heap out-of-bound write vulnerability inside structure in VNC client code that can result remote code execution

undesired_operation.description: Integer overflow in hdr.nSubrects * (4 + (BPP / 8)) causes the size check to be bypassed. The overflowed (small) value passes the RFB_BUFFER_SIZE check, so only a small amount of data is read into client->buffer. However, the for-loop iterates hdr.nSubrects times (the original large value), reading far past the end of client->buffer via ptr increments.
code_snippets: for (i = 0; i < hdr.nSubrects; i++) { pix = *(CARDBPP *)ptr; ptr += BPP/8; x = *ptr++; y = *ptr++; w = *ptr++; h = *ptr++; }
undesired_operation.impact: Heap-based out-of-bounds read past client->buffer, leading to information disclosure or crash. An attacker-controlled malicious VNC server can trigger this.

state.value: attacker-controlled large value causing integer overflow in multiplication
overflowed small value that passes RFB_BUFFER_SIZE check

initial_state.value: unset, to be read from server

step_by_step_execution.description: hdr.nSubrects is read from the RFB server (attacker-controlled) and byte-swapped

out_state.value: attacker-controlled large value, e.g. 0x40000000 for BPP=32

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: With a carefully chosen large hdr.nSubrects, the multiplication overflows to a small value that is <= RFB_BUFFER_SIZE, so the check passes

step_by_step_execution.description: ReadFromRFBServer reads the overflowed small number of bytes into client->buffer
ptr is set to client->buffer, loop iterates hdr.nSubrects (original large) times, reading past buffer

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the root cause of CVE-2018-20020: the integer overflow in the multiplication `hdr.nSubrects * (4 + (BPP / 8))` that bypasses the buffer size check. This is exactly the vulnerability fixed by changing to division-based comparison. The finding labels it as OOB read (CWE-125) while the CVE is classified as OOB write (CWE-787), but the core vulnerability — the integer overflow enabling out-of-bounds access — is the same issue. The OOB read of ptr past client->buffer and subsequent use of those values in GotFillRect can lead to heap corruption (the write aspect), so this is the same vulnerability.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in the multiplication `hdr.nSubrects * (4 + (BPP / 8))` used in the buffer size check allows the check to be bypassed when hdr.nSubrects is a large attacker-controlled value. The fix changes the check to use division (`hdr.nSubrects > RFB_BUFFER_SIZE / (4 + (BPP / 8))`) to avoid the overflow, preventing heap out-of-bounds access.

cve_desc: libmobi is vulnerable to Out-of-bounds Write

undesired_operation.description: When offset is negative, memmove writes 'len' bytes to buf->data + buf->offset without checking that buf->offset + len <= buf->maxlen. This allows writing past the end of the buffer.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow — attacker can corrupt memory beyond the buffer, potentially achieving code execution or causing a crash.

initial_state.value: large enough that buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen

step_by_step_execution.description: aoffset is computed as abs(offset)
source is set to buf->data + buf->offset

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: offset is negative, so we enter the else branch
buf->offset >= aoffset, so the only bounds check in the negative-offset path passes. There is no check for buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen.

relevant_state.value: large, such that buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen

step_by_step_execution.description: source is decremented by aoffset
memmove writes len bytes to buf->data + buf->offset from source, writing past buffer end

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the CVE: when offset is negative, the missing check for buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen allows an out-of-bounds write via memmove. The fix adds exactly this check.

undesired_operation.description: When offset is negative, there is no check that the source read (source + len) stays within the buffer. source starts at buf->data + buf->offset - aoffset, and reading len bytes from it can go past buf->maxlen, causing an out-of-bounds read.
undesired_operation.impact: Out-of-bounds heap read — attacker can read sensitive data from memory beyond the buffer via the memmove source.

initial_state.value: large enough that source + len exceeds buffer

step_by_step_execution.description: aoffset is computed as abs(offset)
source = buf->data + buf->offset - aoffset after the else branch
memmove reads len bytes from source, which can extend past buf->data + buf->maxlen

judgments.reasoning: This is a secondary consequence of the same missing bounds check. The fix (adding buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen) also prevents this OOB read scenario, since it constrains the operation length. It's a valid component of the same vulnerability.

actual_issue_summary: When offset is negative in mobi_buffer_move(), the code only checked that buf->offset >= aoffset (preventing reading before buffer start) but did not check that buf->offset + len <= buf->maxlen. This allowed memmove to write (and read) beyond the end of the allocated buffer, causing an out-of-bounds write (CWE-787). The fix adds the missing check (buf->offset + len > buf->maxlen) in the negative offset branch.

cve_desc: Integer signedness error in the simplestring_addn function in simplestring.c in xmlrpc-epi through 0.54.2, as used in PHP before 5.5.38, 5.6.x before 5.6.24, and 7.x before 7.0.9, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (heap-based buffer overflow) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a long first argument to the PHP xmlrpc_encode_request function.

undesired_operation.description: When add_len is negative, the size check 'target->len + add_len + 1 > target->size' can evaluate to false (skipping reallocation), but memcpy interprets the negative add_len as a very large size_t value, causing a massive heap buffer overflow.
undesired_operation.impact: Heap buffer overflow leading to arbitrary code execution or crash. Attacker controls the length parameter which is passed as a negative int to memcpy, which interprets it as a huge unsigned value.

state.value: negative int value (e.g. -1), interpreted as very large size_t by memcpy

initial_state.value: negative int value from attacker-controlled input

step_by_step_execution.description: add_len is passed as a negative int

step_by_step_execution.reasoning: With add_len negative, the sum target->len + add_len + 1 is smaller than target->len, so it's likely less than target->size, skipping reallocation

step_by_step_execution.description: memcpy is called with add_len implicitly converted to size_t (huge value)

judgments.reasoning: This directly describes the integer signedness error in simplestring_addn that is CVE-2016-6296. The parameter add_len is int, so negative values bypass the size check but cause memcpy to write a huge amount. The fix changes add_len to size_t.
